Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: First the key thing here is that each case patient journey will have a unique combination of patient age and patient gender

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait lets check that first Lets list out all the patient age gender pairs first

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at each events age and gender 45M 62F 28M Wait hold on are there any duplicate age gender pairs Lets see 45M is one 62F is another 28M is the third Oh right All three have distinct age and gender combinations so thats a perfect unique identifier for each case here

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets confirm that because if each patient has a unique age gender then grouping by that makes sense

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets list the 45M patient first Lets go through all events with 45M get their timestamps and activities in order Event 1 Register patient Event 4 Initial triage Event 7 X ray imaging Event 12 Doctor consultation Event 15 Patient discharged

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thats a logical flow register triage x ray for leg issue doctor diagnosis discharge Lets check the order timestamps are in order each step follows the previous Good

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Next the 62F patient Lets list all her events in timestamp order Event 2 Register patient Event 5 Initial triage Event 8 Lab test requested Event 10 Lab test completed Event 11 Doctor consultation Event 14 Prescription created Event 17 Discharged

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thats a logical ER journey for someone with an infection register triage lab work results back doctor sees diagnoses prescribes discharge Timestamps all in order each step makes sense

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now the 28M patient Lets get his events in order Event 3 Register patient Event 6 Doctor consultation Event 9 Surgery preparation Event 13 Surgery performed Event 16 Recovery room Event 18 Discharged

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thats exactly a scheduled surgery patient Register for surgery pre op consult prep for surgery surgery recovery discharge with physio followup All timestamps are in order each step follows logically

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets cross verify each events patient attributes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 45 year old Male Events 1 4 7 12 15 Total 5 events all 45M timestamps in order flow makes sense

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 62 year old Female Events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 7 events all 62F flow register triage lab work ordered results back doctor diagnoses infection prescribes antibiotics discharge

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 28 year old Male Events 3 6 9 13 16 18 6 events all 28M flow scheduled knee surgery pre op consult prep for surgery surgery done recover in recovery room discharge with physio

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait lets make sure we didnt mix any up

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The departments per case 45M is mostly ER except x ray in radiology which is normal 62F is ER and lab which is normal 28M is all Surgery department which makes sense

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Dr Smith is the ER on call doctor sees both ER patients Thats not a problem

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The events are interleaved across cases which is what the problem says

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check the discharge times 45M discharged first 62F next 28M last That makes sense

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Is there any event that doesnt fit any of the three cases 5 plus 7 plus 6 equals 18 which is all the events Perfect no leftovers

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Timestamps per case are all in ascending order Case 45M increasing Case 62F increasing Case 28M increasing

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Is there any possible ambiguity Is there any event that could belong to more than one case Event 12 could not be 28M or 62F Event 13 only fits 28M Event 7 only fits 45M

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The only possible grouping is by age gender which gives exactly three cases all with logical event flows
